title: Hieracium ivigtutense
taxonomy: Hieracium ivigtutense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Within the genus Hieracium, H. ivigtutense is grouped with other hawkweeds, which are characterized by their yellow or white flowers and hairy leaves. The species is also classified as a member of the subgenus Hieracium, which is a subset of the genus that is primarily found in the Northern Hemisphere. Further classification includes its specific epithet ivigtutense, which refers to its discovery in Greenland.

intro: Hieracium ivigtutense, also known as Arctic hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium ivigtutense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and polyphenols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium ivigtutense include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness the plant's medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
